mechanism: If DOGE delivers $50-200B in cuts (workforce/program cuts rather than waste elimination), the asymmetric contraction multiplier (1.2-1.8x) means GDP impact exceeds the nominal savings, potentially worsening the debt/GDP ratio. The multiplier literature gap on workforce reductions adds uncertainty.
regime_dependence: Depends on composition of actual cuts (workforce vs. transfer vs. procurement). Workforce reduction multipliers are underresearched relative to transfer/tax change multipliers.
